---
title: "Publishing an HTML File to Hugging Face Spaces"
subtitle: "Step-by-step cheat sheet"
author: "IBM 6300 / IBM 6540"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
---

## One-Time Setup (do this only once per project)

### 1. Create a Hugging Face Access Token
1. Go to **huggingface.co** → log in
2. Profile picture (top right) → **Settings** → **Access Tokens**
3. Click **New token** → name it anything → set role to **Write**
4. Copy the token and save it somewhere safe — you won't see it again

### 2. Create a New Space
1. Go to **huggingface.co/new-space**
2. Give it a name (e.g. `huff-model`)
3. Set SDK to **Static**
4. Click **Create Space**

### 3. Clone the Space to Your Computer
In your terminal, navigate to where you want the folder, then:
```bash
git clone https://huggingface.co/spaces/yourusername/yourspacename
cd yourspacename
```

### 4. Configure git to Store Credentials (one time only)
```bash
git config --global credential.helper store
```

---

## Every Time You Want to Publish / Update

### Step 1 — Copy your HTML file into the cloned folder
- Name it **`index.html`** — this is what Hugging Face serves by default
- If your file has a different name, rename it to `index.html`

### Step 2 — Open the folder in your terminal
Make sure your terminal is in the right folder:
```bash
cd "full\path\to\yourspacename"
```
Or open the project in Positron — the terminal will be set automatically.

### Step 3 — Check what has changed
```bash
git status
```
You should see your `index.html` listed as modified or new.

### Step 4 — Stage, commit, and push
```bash
git add index.html
git commit -m "update"
git push
```
When prompted for credentials:

- **Username**: your Hugging Face username
- **Password**: paste your **access token** (not your actual password)

### Step 5 — View your page
```
https://huggingface.co/spaces/yourusername/yourspacename
```

---

## Quick Diagnostic Commands

| Problem | Command to run | What to look for |
|---|---|---|
| Am I in the right folder? | `git remote -v` | Should show `huggingface.co` URL |
| Were my changes committed? | `git log --oneline -3` | Your latest commit message |
| Are there uncommitted changes? | `git status` | Should say "nothing to commit" after pushing |

---

## Common Mistakes to Avoid

- **Wrong folder in terminal** — always check `git remote -v` first to confirm you're in the right repo
- **Entering password instead of access token** — the password field always takes the token, not your HF password
- **File not named index.html** — Hugging Face Static Spaces won't know what to serve otherwise
- **Forgetting to `git add` before `git commit`** — changes not staged won't be included in the commit

---

## Credentials Troubleshooting (Windows 11)

If git keeps asking for credentials even after entering them:
```bash
git config --global credential.helper store
```

If you entered the wrong credentials and need to clear them:

1. Search **Credential Manager** in Start menu
2. Click **Windows Credentials** tab
3. Find and remove the `git:https://huggingface.co` entry

If Windows Credentials are disabled by your system administrator:
```bash
git config --global credential.helper store
```
This saves credentials to `C:\Users\yourusername\.git-credentials` instead, bypassing the restriction.
